candidates.why: This D&A row appears in the operating-overhead section alongside Central administration, not within or beneath Property costs / Cost of Revenues. Relative to the selected COGS bucket (Property costs plus Balancing Item - Operating Expenses), it does not clearly belong to COGS.

This row appears in the central-administration/operating-overhead area rather than within Property costs / Cost of Revenues. Relative to the selected COGS bucket, it does not appear to be COGS-related D&A.

variables.notes: No explicit COGS-specific D&A row is visible above the financial section. The visible D&A rows appear associated with central administration / SG&A, so they should not be added to COGS.

candidates.why: This is a separate operating expense line at the same level as Central administration and Labor & Related Expenses in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses. Relative to the selected XSGA bucket, it appears to sit alongside rather than inside those selected rows, so it should be added separately.

This appears to be the later-period label for the same separate SG&A-related D&A line, shown alongside central administration rather than as a sub-component of the selected XSGA rows. It should be added separately for periods where this label is used.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket contains Central administration and SG&A labor rows. The D&A lines are presented separately in the same operating section and do not appear nested within those selected rows. Both labels are included because they appear to represent the same separate SG&A D&A line under different period labeling, not simultaneous components to sum within the same period.
